data.title: Additional Considerations Required While Assessing ASM International's (AMS:ASM) Strong Earnings
data.description:
ASM International NV (AMS:ASM) just reported some strong earnings, and the market reacted accordingly with a healthy uplift in the share price. We did some analysis and think that investors are missing some details hidden beneath the profit numbers.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Zooming In On ASM International's Earnings

One key financial ratio used to measure how well a company converts its profit to free cash flow (FCF) is the accrual ratio. The accrual ratio subtracts the FCF from the profit for a given period, and divides the result by the average operating assets of the company over that time. You could think of the accrual ratio from cashflow as the 'non-FCF profit ratio'.

That means a negative accrual ratio is a good thing, because it shows that the company is bringing in more free cash flow than its profit would suggest. While having an accrual ratio above zero is of little concern, we do think it's worth noting when a company has a relatively high accrual ratio. To quote a 2014 paper by Lewellen and Resutek, "firms with higher accruals tend to be less profitable in the future".

For the year to March 2026, ASM International had an accrual ratio of 0.24. We can therefore deduce that its free cash flow fell well short of covering its statutory profit. In fact, it had free cash flow of €282m in the last year, which was a lot less than its statutory profit of €991.1m. ASM International's free cash flow actually declined over the last year, but it may bounce back next year, since free cash flow is often more volatile than accounting profits. Having said that, there is more to the story. The accrual ratio is reflecting the impact of unusual items on statutory profit, at least in part. One positive for ASM International shareholders is that it's accrual ratio was significantly better last year, providing reason to believe that it may return to stronger cash conversion in the future. As a result, some shareholders may be looking for stronger cash conversion in the current year.

See our latest analysis for ASM International

That might leave you wondering what analysts are forecasting in terms of future profitability. Luckily, you can click here to see an interactive graph depicting future profitability, based on their estimates.

The Impact Of Unusual Items On Profit

The fact that the company had unusual items boosting profit by €199m, in the last year, probably goes some way to explain why its accrual ratio was so weak. While we like to see profit increases, we tend to be a little more cautious when unusual items have made a big contribution. When we analysed the vast majority of listed companies worldwide, we found that significant unusual items are often not repeated. And that's as you'd expect, given these boosts are described as 'unusual'. Assuming those unusual items don't show up again in the current year, we'd thus expect profit to be weaker next year (in the absence of business growth, that is).

Our Take On ASM International's Profit Performance

Summing up, ASM International received a nice boost to profit from unusual items, but could not match its paper profit with free cash flow. Considering all this we'd argue ASM International's profits probably give an overly generous impression of its sustainable level of profitability. With this in mind, we wouldn't consider investing in a stock unless we had a thorough understanding of the risks. To help with this, we've discovered 2 warning signs (1 doesn't sit too well with us!) that you ought to be aware of before buying any shares in ASM International.

Our examination of ASM International has focussed on certain factors that can make its earnings look better than they are. And, on that basis, we are somewhat skeptical. But there is always more to discover if you are capable of focussing your mind on minutiae. For example, many people consider a high return on equity as an indication of favorable business economics, while others like to 'follow the money' and search out stocks that insiders are buying. data.title: Merck Broadens Growth Story With Heart Campaign And New Drug Approvals
data.description:
• Merck (NYSE:MRK) has launched its Playing with Heart cardiovascular awareness campaign in partnership with several professional baseball teams.
  • The company recently received European Union approval for ENFLONSIA for RSV prevention in infants.
  • Merck also secured US FDA approval for IDVYNSO, a new HIV treatment regimen.

For investors watching Merck, these updates come as the stock trades around $110.23, with a 1 year return of 37.3% and a 5 year return of 78.1%. While the share price has seen a 7 day return of 5.9% decline and a 30 day return of 7.9% decline, the year to date return of 3.6% and 3 year return of 3.9% highlight a mixed recent picture alongside longer term performance that has been relatively strong.

Merck's new cardiovascular campaign and the fresh approvals in RSV prevention and HIV treatment point to a broader focus across major disease areas beyond oncology. For investors, these developments may be useful to watch as part of assessing how NYSE:MRK is positioning its portfolio across vaccines, infectious disease and chronic conditions over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Merck by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Merck.

5 things going right for Merck that this headline doesn't cover.

For investors, Merck’s Playing with Heart campaign sits alongside the recent ENFLONSIA and IDVYNSO approvals as part of a broader push beyond oncology into vaccines, infectious disease and cardiometabolic care. The baseball partnership is an awareness effort rather than a revenue driver, but it can support Merck’s cardiovascular ambitions by strengthening relationships with patients, physicians and payers around LDL cholesterol and heart risk. ENFLONSIA gives Merck a European Union approved RSV product for infants, adding to an area where large peers such as Pfizer and Sanofi are also active. IDVYNSO does something similar in HIV, where Gilead Sciences and ViiV Healthcare are key competitors, by offering a non integrase inhibitor, tenofovir free option for virologically suppressed adults. Together, these moves suggest Merck is working to spread future earnings across more therapeutic areas at a time when Keytruda patent timing is a focus. The key question for you is how much weight to put on this diversification versus the execution risk that comes with launching and educating multiple markets at once.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Merck Narrative

  • The Playing with Heart campaign and new approvals in RSV and HIV support the idea in the community narrative that Merck is building a wider set of growth drivers beyond oncology and vaccines tied to a single brand.
  • At the same time, adding cardiology and HIV initiatives increases Merck’s to do list, which could challenge the narrative assumption that the company can efficiently scale many late stage assets in parallel.
  • The narrative focuses heavily on oncology, CAPVAXIVE and WINREVAIR, so the specific contribution from ENFLONSIA, IDVYNSO and cardiovascular awareness work may not be fully reflected in how future earnings and risks are framed.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Merck to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts highlight that Merck carries a high level of debt, which can limit flexibility if multiple new launches in RSV, HIV and cardiovascular care do not meet expectations.
  • ⚠️ The company still faces competition from global players such as Pfizer, Gilead Sciences and Sanofi, so new products could experience pricing pressure or slower uptake than planned.
  • 🎁 Merck pays a dividend yield around 3.08%, and has a history of maintaining and growing that payout, which some investors may see as support while the product mix evolves.
  • 🎁 The stock is flagged by Simply Wall St as trading well below an internal fair value estimate, and earnings are forecast to grow, which some readers may view as compensation for the execution and patent related risks.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth watching how quickly ENFLONSIA rolls out across European markets, how IDVYNSO is adopted as a switch option for virologically suppressed HIV patients, and whether the Playing with Heart campaign leads to additional cardiology partnerships or products. Updates to Merck’s guidance around the mix of oncology, vaccines, infectious disease and cardiovascular revenue will help show whether this broader therapeutic focus is gaining real traction. Competitor moves from Pfizer, Gilead Sciences and others in RSV and HIV will also shape how investors assess Merck’s share in these markets over time.

data.title: Boeing’s MQ-25A Milestone Puts Naval Autonomy In Investor Focus
data.description:
• Boeing's MQ-25A Stingray completed its first operational test flight with the U.S. Navy.
  • The flight marks a key milestone for autonomous carrier based aerial refueling.
  • The program positions Boeing in an important role within naval unmanned systems.

Boeing, listed as NYSE:BA, is drawing fresh attention from defense focused investors after this MQ-25A milestone. The company’s current share price is $231.33, with the stock up 2.8% over the past week and 21.4% over the past month, and a 1 year return of 26.9%. These returns place the MQ-25A news within a period where the market has been receptive to the Boeing story.

For investors, the first successful MQ-25A test flight highlights Boeing’s role in unmanned and autonomous defense programs, alongside its existing commercial and defense activities. The progress of this program and future contract activity are important areas to monitor for anyone tracking how much of Boeing’s value may be linked to defense and advanced systems over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Boeing by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Boeing.

3 things going right for Boeing that this headline doesn't cover.

data.title: Nebius Weighs AI21 Labs Deal As CoreWeave Capacity Tests Growth Story
data.description:
• Nebius Group (NasdaqGS:NBIS) is in active talks to acquire Israeli AI firm AI21 Labs after Nvidia exited earlier negotiations.
  • The company is also expanding its AI infrastructure footprint through new data center capacity contracts with cloud specialist CoreWeave.
  • These developments come as Nebius shares trade at $144.96, following a 61.2% return year to date and a very large 1 year gain.

Nebius Group, ticker NasdaqGS:NBIS, is pursuing AI21 Labs at a time when its own share price sits at $144.96 and recent returns have been strong. The stock is up 61.2% year to date and around 7x over the past 1 year, which puts extra attention on how new AI assets and infrastructure deals might shape the company’s next phase.

For investors, the AI21 Labs talks and new CoreWeave contracts highlight how Nebius is positioning its AI software stack alongside capacity in key data center markets. The outcome of the acquisition negotiations, together with execution on these infrastructure agreements, may influence the company’s future product mix, customer reach, and how the durability of recent share price gains is perceived.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Nebius Group by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Nebius Group.

2 things going right for Nebius Group that this headline doesn't cover.

This potential acquisition of AI21 Labs sits alongside growing infrastructure commitments and helps fill out Nebius Group’s AI stack. AI21 Labs focuses on large language models and enterprise AI tools, which could give Nebius more of its own higher level software to run on the GPU capacity it is already lining up with partners such as CoreWeave and TD SYNNEX. That matters if Nebius wants to capture not just spending on raw compute, but also spending on applied AI use cases where customers compare it with Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud. At the same time, more data center build outs and a sizeable software acquisition would add to capital needs and execution complexity, which investors are already watching given analyst comments on funding, earnings quality, and valuation checks. The key question is whether combining AI21 Labs, new CoreWeave contracts, and existing deployments like TD SYNNEX’s reserved NVIDIA clusters on Nebius AI Cloud can support durable usage and revenue without stretching the balance sheet or increasing operational risk too far.

How This Fits Into The Nebius Group Narrative

  • The AI21 Labs talks and new CoreWeave data center capacity support the existing narrative that Nebius is building full stack AI infrastructure backed by large partners and long term contracts.
  • Adding another AI asset and more contracted capacity could amplify earlier concerns in the narrative about high capital requirements and the need to secure power, funding, and regulatory approvals across multiple regions.
  • The possibility that Nebius could pair in house foundation models from AI21 Labs with its own Aether cloud upgrade and TD SYNNEX distribution appears to be only partly reflected in the narrative, which focuses more on large hyperscale contracts.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Nebius Group to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Nebius already carries a high level of non cash earnings, and adding AI21 Labs plus more data center commitments could make it harder for you to track how quickly accounting profit converts into cash as the AI business scales.
  • 🎁 Revenue is forecast to grow 42.76% a year, and AI21 Labs plus CoreWeave contracts give Nebius more routes to attract AI workloads, from foundation models and enterprise agents to GPU intensive training and inference.
  • 🎁 The combination of Nebius AI Cloud 3.5, TD SYNNEX’s reserved NVIDIA clusters, and potential ownership of AI21 Labs could make Nebius more relevant for enterprises that want an alternative to the largest cloud providers for production grade AI projects.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on three things. First, whether Nebius and AI21 Labs reach a binding deal and how any purchase is funded, since that will inform dilution and balance sheet risk. Second, how quickly CoreWeave capacity and TD SYNNEX’s reserved NVIDIA clusters on Nebius AI Cloud convert into active workloads, not just announcements. data.title: Kinden (TSE:1944) Margin Gain To 9.3% Challenges Premium Valuation Narrative
data.description:
Kinden (TSE:1944) has closed FY 2026 with fourth quarter revenue of ¥251.5b and basic EPS of ¥147.42, while trailing 12 month revenue stands at ¥750.7b with EPS of ¥350.53, setting a clear earnings season benchmark for investors. Over recent reporting periods, total revenue has ranged from ¥141.4b to ¥251.5b per quarter and basic EPS has moved between ¥22.01 and ¥147.42, giving a tangible sense of how the earnings base has scaled into the current trailing net profit margin profile. With earnings quality described as high and margins now framed by a 9.3% trailing net profit margin versus 6.7% a year earlier, the latest results put profitability and earnings momentum at the center of the story.

47% earnings growth reshapes profit base

  • Kinden’s trailing 12 month net income of ¥69,447 million sits alongside a 47% year on year earnings increase and a 5 year average earnings growth rate of 18.8%, so the current profit level is backed by a multi year growth record rather than a one off spike.
  • What stands out for the bullish view is how the higher profit base lines up with recent quarters, with FY 2026 quarterly net income ranging from ¥4,365 million in Q1 to ¥29,190 million in Q4. This supports the idea of a larger earnings engine but also shows that seasonality and timing of work can still create wide swings across the year.
    • Supporters who focus on the 47% trailing 12 month earnings growth and the improved margin profile can point to this as evidence that the higher earnings level is now embedded in the business, even though Q1 was much lighter than later quarters.
    • Cautious investors may look at that same spread in quarterly net income and EPS, from ¥4,365 million and ¥22.01 in Q1 up to ¥29,190 million and ¥147.42 in Q4, and see a reminder that the full year strength relies on consistently strong execution across the project cycle rather than just one strong period.

Net profit margin rises to 9.3%

  • The trailing net profit margin of 9.3% compares with 6.7% a year earlier, and sits on top of trailing 12 month revenue of ¥750,742 million and net income of ¥69,447 million. This gives a clearer picture of how much profit is being kept from each yen of sales over the last year.
  • Critics who take a more bearish stance often question how durable higher margins are for construction related businesses, and the move from a 6.7% to 9.3% trailing net margin gives both sides something to point to, because it shows better profitability over the last year but still leaves open how that margin might hold up if project mix or pricing shift.
    • On one hand, bears can argue that a margin near 9% is above the prior 6.7% level and may reflect a period of particularly favourable projects, so they might worry that margins could trend closer to prior levels if competitive pressure increases.
    • On the other hand, the fact that this higher margin lines up with multi year earnings growth of 18.8% per year suggests that improved profitability has not appeared in isolation, which challenges the idea that recent margin strength is purely temporary without any structural support.
Over the last year of margin gains and faster earnings growth, many investors are asking whether this is a one off or the start of a new chapter for the business. That is exactly the kind of context you will find in the broader community discussion around the stock, 📊 Read the what the Community is saying about Kinden..

Premium P/E and DCF gap

  • Kinden trades on a trailing P/E of 22.6x versus a peer average of 19.6x and a Japan construction industry average of 12.1x, and at a share price of ¥7,932 compared with a DCF fair value of ¥6,021.88. Both the multiple and the DCF comparison show the stock at a premium to these reference points.
  • What may challenge bullish investors here is the tension between strong historical profit growth and a higher starting valuation, because earnings are forecast to grow about 8.23% per year and revenue about 4.9% per year while the stock already trades above both peer P/E levels and the DCF fair value. This means the case for further upside relies on how comfortable investors are with paying that premium based on the company’s track record and the forecast growth profile.
    • Supporters might argue that five year earnings growth of 18.8% per year and a 47% increase over the last year justify the 22.6x P/E, even if forecasts point to more moderate future growth.
    • Skeptics can counter that with growth forecasts below the broader Japan market references and a share price that sits above the ¥6,021.88 DCF fair value estimate, plus a more volatile share price over the past three months, the valuation leaves limited room for disappointment.
